Title: Stringent response regulators (p)ppGpp and DksA positively regulate virulence and host adaptation of Xanthomonas citri

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: DksA/TraR family; C-terminal zinc finger domain
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Deletion mutant; complementation; RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; GUS promoter assay; pathogenicity assay; hypersensitive response assay; motility assay; siderophore CAS assay
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively regulates nutrient uptake/host adaptation traits; promotes expression of TonB-dependent transporter genes and histidine metabolism genes; represses xss siderophore cluster and siderophore production; required for full virulence and in planta growth
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Siderophore supports iron acquisition; histidine supports amino acid metabolism; sucrose uptake supports bacterial nutrition and host adaptation
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Bacterial extracellular milieu/iron-scavenging halo for siderophore; NA for others
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Function studied under hrp-inducing XVM2 medium and during host colonization-related conditions
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Nutrient limitation/stringent response conditions; XVM2 hrp-inducing medium; plant host apoplastic environment; iron-limited CAS medium with 2,2'-dipyridyl
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. dksA deletion abolished canker symptom development and reduced in planta growth. 2. DksA positively regulated TonB-dependent transporter genes and histidine metabolism-related genes, linking it to nutrient uptake/adaptation. 3. DksA negatively regulated xss cluster expression and increased loss of DksA caused elevated siderophore production.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts as a stringent response cofactor; also positively regulates T3SS/T2SS and represses tRNA/ribosome/flagellar and type IV pilus genes, but those are virulence/background outputs rather than nutrient terminal products.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Deletion reduces motility and virulence; regulatory effects are pleiotropic and may alter secretion, growth and iron homeostasis.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Primarily a pathogen control target rather than crop nutrient engineering target; disrupting DksA-regulated nutrient uptake/iron homeostasis may reduce bacterial fitness and virulence.

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Bifunctional (p)ppGpp synthase/hydrolase; SYNTH, HD, TGS and ACT domains
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Double mutant analysis (ΔspoTΔrelA); complementation with spoT; RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; GUS promoter assay; pathogenicity assay; hypersensitive response assay; motility assay
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Via (p)ppGpp homeostasis, positively regulates TonB-dependent transporter genes and histidine metabolism genes and supports virulence/host adaptation; contributes to nutrient uptake
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports amino acid metabolism and nutrient acquisition during host adaptation
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Function studied in XVM2 medium and host-associated conditions
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. spoT function, assessed in ΔspoTΔrelA, was required for full canker development and in planta growth. 2. SpoT-dependent stringent response positively regulated TonB-dependent transporter genes and histidine metabolism. 3. Regulation of siderophore/xss genes differed from DksA, indicating distinct control of iron homeostasis.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: spoT single deletion could not be obtained, consistent with essentiality concerns from (p)ppGpp hyperaccumulation; conclusions derive from ΔspoTΔrelA and spoT complementation.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Strong pleiotropic effects on growth, secretion, motility and virulence traits.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential antimicrobial target to impair pathogen nutrient uptake and adaptation.

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: (p)ppGpp synthase/hydrolase family protein with SYNTH, HD, TGS and ACT domains
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Deletion mutant; comparison with ΔspoTΔrelA in pathogenicity and growth assays
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: relA single deletion had little to no effect on pathogenicity; included as component of (p)ppGpp-deficient background rather than decisive standalone nutrient regulator
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. relA was identified as a stringent response regulator gene. 2. relA single deletion did not noticeably affect virulence, likely because SpoT retained weak synthetase activity. 3. Its main nutrient-metabolism relevance in this paper is as part of the ΔspoTΔrelA (p)ppGpp-deficient background.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not a primary core nutrient-metabolism gene for standalone causal effect in results.

Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: RNA polymerase-binding transcription factor/stringent response regulator
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional regulation in concert with (p)ppGpp; activation of nutrient uptake/metabolism genes and repression of iron acquisition/siderophore genes under tested conditions
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: TonB-dependent transporter genes including suxA/suxB/suxC locus-associated genes; histidine metabolism genes; xss gene cluster including mhpE/xsuA/xssA-xssE
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates many TonB-dependent transporter and histidine metabolism genes; represses xss siderophore biosynthesis/utilization genes
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Nutrient uptake; amino acid metabolism; iron acquisition/homeostasis
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent controller of nutrient uptake and iron homeostasis during host adaptation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Deletion mutant; complementation; RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; GUS assay of mhpE promoter; CAS siderophore assay
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes nutrient acquisition capacity and host adaptation while repressing siderophore overproduction; required for full virulence
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Extracellular halo on CAS medium for siderophore
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Iron scavenging and nutrient acquisition for survival in host apoplast
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Nutrition deprivation; host-associated stress; stringent response-inducing conditions
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: XVM2 hrp-inducing medium; host apoplastic conditions; iron-limited CAS assay
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DksA is a central stringent response regulator required for full pathogenicity and growth in planta. 2. It positively regulates TBDT and histidine metabolism genes, supporting nutrient uptake. 3. It represses the xss siderophore cluster, and loss of DksA increases siderophore production, indicating a role in iron homeostasis.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Broad pleiotropy across virulence, motility, secretion and growth traits.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful antimicrobial target to weaken pathogen nutrient acquisition and host adaptation rather than host nutritional improvement.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Assessed during growth in XVM2 medium; biologically relevant under nutrient-limiting/host adaptation conditions
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoter used for complementation; mhpE promoter tested by GUS
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Differentially regulates iron-homeostasis genes compared with (p)ppGpp.

Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: Alarmone metabolism enzyme and stringent response regulator
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Bifunctional (p)ppGpp synthase/hydrolase with SYNTH, HD, TGS, ACT domains
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: TonB-dependent transporter genes including sux locus; histidine metabolism genes; xss siderophore cluster
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates TBDT and histidine metabolism genes; relative to DksA shows opposite tendency on xss cluster under tested conditions
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Nutrient uptake; amino acid metabolism; iron acquisition/homeostasis
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major stringent-response controller supporting nutrient uptake and host adaptation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: ΔspoTΔrelA mutant; spoT complementation; RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; GUS assay
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports nutrient uptake-related transcriptional programs and full virulence; affects iron homeostasis genes differently from DksA
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports host adaptation through nutrient and iron acquisition
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Nutrient deprivation and stress/stringent response cues
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Functional interaction with DksA and RNA polymerase via (p)ppGpp signaling
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. (p)ppGpp deficiency in ΔspoTΔrelA reduced virulence and in planta growth. 2. SpoT/(p)ppGpp positively regulated TBDT and histidine metabolism genes. 3. xss siderophore cluster regulation diverged from DksA, suggesting nuanced control of iron homeostasis.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Broad pleiotropic effects on motility, secretion and growth; spoT single deletion appears not recoverable.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for pathogen suppression through stringent response disruption.
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native spoT promoter used in complementation
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Evidence inferred from ΔspoTΔrelA because spoT single mutant could not be obtained.
